IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified 15 high voltage poles that need replacing and upgrading. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. During this time, you were connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily. This work is taking place in and around North Elham in Folkstone and Hythe. A road closure will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message:
Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the Messaging Team here at UK Power Networks. 

You may notice that we have less of our teams, vehicles and partners in the Colworth Road area after today. I wanted to offer you my personal reassurance that even though we have less physical presence on site, our partners, Haste, will still be working to visit each property to repair or replace damaged equipment.

Should you need to speak to Haste, they can be contacted on 01730 897100. As this will be the last message from my team, I wanted to extend a final and sincere apology for the inconvenience caused by what has been a very uncommon and complex fault with our network. Thank you.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Good evening. Just a final update to advise that our Customer Champions, the food van and Customer Support vehicle have all now left site.

They will be returning to the area from 9am tomorrow morning and we will let you know. If you have any further questions or concerns this evening though, please do call us on 105 and we will do our best to provide support. Sent at 20:23 by Will.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Good evening. Just a quick update to advise that our Customer Champions will be leaving the area at 20:00 and then return tomorrow morning from approx 09:00.

They are still based in the Claremont Road at the moment though so please do visit them if you need any information or support at all. If you are unable to make it to them though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105 or simply 105 and we will be able to get a message to them.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Will at 14:07 - We can now confirm that all power in the Huntingdon PE29 area should be back on. Our engineers have diverted power around underground network fault to get the majority of customers back on. However, due to the location and complexity of the issue, we have also used temporary generators to restore power to the last of the affected properties. This will provide power until our engineers plan in and safely complete the full repair, which may take a few days.

We're really sorry for any disruption this unexpected power cut caused. If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have just called in to advise us an additional generator has now arrived on site and our engineers are in the process of connecting this to the network.

Unfortunately, it's going to take us past our current time frame to complete this safely, so we're now working towards between 13:30 and 14:30 to have power back on.

We can appreciate how frustrating and disruptive being without power can be, and our staff are working as fast as they can. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to speak to our team, but I'll let you know further news as it comes in from site. Sent by Luke at 12:35.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:

Unfortunately, it's going to take us past our current time frame to complete this safely, so we're now working towards between 13:30 and 14:30 to have power back on.

We can appreciate how frustrating and disruptive being without power can be, and our staff are working as fast as they can. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to speak to our team, but I'll let you know further news as it comes in from site. Sent by Luke at 12:35.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi, this is Stuart from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I'm sorry for the power cut today; I know the lights going off can be disruptive.

We know that some customers affected by todays power cut may have had other issues recently, and I appreciate this is frustrating. High voltage cabling can cover a wide area, so sometimes properties very close to you may have had power cuts that you didn't experience.

I know you may not have had any previous power cuts, but if you have, we often get progress updates from our field staff management about investigations into repeat issues, and our Customer Services team will try to contact everyone affected by them to explain what's happening.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Stuart at 09:01 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our overhead cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Sent by Luke at 09:21 - Our engineers have restored power to all 785 properties in the Bedford, Sandy and St Neots area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around the underground fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Luke at 08:51 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground fault that initially interrupted power to 745 properties. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 10:00 and 11:00 but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve this issue safely; we're pleased that our engineers were able restore the power within the timeframe we had estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by Stuart at 12:27.

We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. Sent by Stuart at 10:59 - Thanks for your patience this morning.

Our staff are checking our equipment throughout the area to pinpoint the issue on our overhead cabling network. In addition to this, they are liaising closely with our control room to find safe and reliable ways to divert power around any problems and get everyone restored.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
